s.m. more: n. m. S. रूप, Pālī, rūpam . 1. Face; features; countenance; character ( bhes , 1, 2, 4.). 2. Beauty; bloom; radiance. 1. Āṅkhan agāṛī sūp, tā se baṛā rūp . Riddle. Ans. Palak . Eyelashes. Before the eyes two winnowing fans which beauty great confer. 2. Rūp roë, bhāg khāë . Prov. Beauty weeps while Fortune enjoys. 3. Hāth līë ārsī rūp ko tole . Padmāvat. Her looking glass in hand her beauty she doth weigh. 4. Nahāne se niklā ajab uskā rūp, Nikal āë badlī se jis tarah dhūp . From out the water as she came her beauty Mīr Hasan. shone, As shines the sun emerged from clouds the earth upon. 5. Sohāgin sīs sab apnā guṅdhāveṅ, Basan, bhūkan se rūp apnā banāveṅ . Wom. song. 6. Ek nār ne kīyā singār, māno rūp dīyā Kartār . 3. Case; condition. rūp badal'nā , v. a. To change one's countenance ( bhes badalnā ). rūp bigāṛnā , v. a. 1. To disfigure; deform; deface. 2. def: (cf. rust), prop. n. m. Rustam, the son of Zāl, the most renowned of Persian heroes; (fig.) a hero, a brave man
more: n. 1. One of the twelve champions of Persia. 2. ( Rustam khāṅ ) A brave man; a hero. Ek tum hī to Rustam ho! rustam kā sālā , n. m. A braggart. Ajab naqshā hai dunyā kā, ajab ālam hai ālam kā, Ki ab to jo rizālā hai vohā sālā hai Rustam kā . Zahūr. chhupā Rustam . A Hercules in disguise; a dark horse.

n. prop. One of the twelve champions of Persia. His combat with Isfandyār is famous in the east; it lasted two days: and, as the brazen body of his enemy was proof against his arrows, he at last killed him with his mace. He was son of Zāl, who was son of Sām, son of Narīmān. He was general to Kaikāʼūs, and considered by the Persians descended from Māmūn, son of Benjamin, son of Jacob. He overcame Afrāsiyāb, king of Turkistān, who was joined by the Indians and Tartars: nevertheless, he fell into disgrace with his master, principally on account of his rejecting the religion of Zoroaster; and, it was upon this occasion that he killed Kaikāʼūsʼs son Isfandyār above mentioned. He was killed by the wound of an arrow in an engagement with Bahman (or Artaxerxes Longimanus) son of Isfandyār. Hence a hero is called Rustam.
subs: s.m. lit. 'Rustam's brother-in-law'; a boaster, a braggart.

more: n. f. Pl. of رسم Fees; duties; perquisites. rusūm-dār , G. G. n. m. The holder of certain fees or perquisites. rusūm-i-sarkār . Stamp duties. rusūm-i-adālat . Court fees. rusūm-i-gair-mahkūmā . Arbitrary cesses. rusūm-i-karāo ; H. karāo-rīt . The custom of karāo , q. v. The marriage of a widow with the brother of a deceased husband, which especially obtains among the Jāts, Gūjars, Ahirs, and other inferior tribes in the N. W. P. rusūm-i-gaṭhṛī , G. G.; H. gaṭhṛī-kar , n. f. Fees on bales and packages. rusūm-i-mohtarafa , G. G. Taxes levied on trades and professions.
s. f. (plur. of رسم) Customs, &c. a fee, duties, taxes, postage of letters.

def: s.f. pl. of and = rusūm, q.v. s.m. One who holds particular fees or perquisites
more: s. f. plur. of رسوم rusūm, q. v.

def: adj. Dishonoured, disgraced, opprobrious, infamous, ignominious (cf. rū-siyāh); — s.m. Infamy, &c. ( = ruswāʼī)
more: adj. Dishonored; disgraced; infamous ( bad-nām ). rusvā k. , v. a. To dishonor; disgrace ( bad-nām k .). rusvā h. , v. n. To be disgraced; to get a bad name.
1. adj. Infamous, ignominious, dishonoured, disgraced. 2. s. m. Opprobrium, disgrace. رسواي عالم ruswā-e-āﻌlam, Infamous throughout the world.
subs: adj. Infamous throughout the world, of world-wide infamy
v.t. To dishonour, disgrace, render infamous

def: s. f. Ignominy, infamy, dishonour, disgrace, disrepute, &c.
s.f. Dishonour, disrepute, disgrace, infamy, opprobrium, ignominy.
s.m. Cry, noise; hum; note, song (of birds).
prop. n. f. Ruth; the Book of Ruth.
s.m. Fresh ripe dates (pl. art̤ab).
s.m. Herbage, green pasture, green trees.

ety: for A. رتبة, n. of un. fr. رتب 'to become stable or firm,' &c.
def: s.m. A step, stair; station, standing, honour, rank, condition, quality, degree, dignity; high station or dignity, &c.; distinction
more: n. m. رتب the steps of stairs. 1. Step; honor (پايه 3.). Māṅ bāp kā baṛā rutbah hai . 2. Weight; importance; distinction. 3. Class; quality. 4. Capacity (of soil). rutbah baṛhnā , v. n. To advance in dignity, etc. rutbah ghaṭānā , v. a. To degrade or reduce one.
(from رتب) s. m. Rank, dignity, degree, stair, step.
subs: adj. Of high rank or station, noble, distinguished, eminent.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رتبه (rutba), from Arabic رُتْبَة (rutba).

ety: perf. part of rūṭhnā, and = S. रुष्ट+कः
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Irritated, offended, displeased, angry, estranged, cool.
wety: From Sanskrit रुष्ट (ruṣṭa).

def: v. a. S. रुष्ट Caus. of rūṭhnā , q. v.
v.t. To offend, displease, vex, irritate.
s.f. Mutual coolness, misunderstanding.
s. f. Mutual coolness, misunderstanding.
v.n. = روٿهنا rūṭhnā, q.v.

ety: rūṭh˚ = Prk. रुट्ठ(इ), fr. रुट्ठ = S. रुष्ट p.p.p. of rt. रुष्
def: v.n. To be irritated, be vexed, be offended or displeased, to take offence or umbrage; to quarrel, to have a misunderstanding (with a friend), to become estranged, to be cool.
more: v. n. To have a misunderstanding with a friend, to be cool, to quarrel, to be angry or displeased.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀭𑀼𑀝𑁆𑀞 (ruṭṭha, “angry”) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix), the first element of which is from Sanskrit रुष्ट (ruṣṭa).

def: v. n. 1. To be huffed, offended, or displeased. 2. To pout; sulk; to be in the dumps. rūṭh'ā , adj. Huffed; offended; displeased. 1. Rūṭhe ko manāë nahīṅ, phaṭe ko silāë nahīṅ, to kām kyoṅkar chale? Prov. The offended needs must be appeased, what's torn must mended be, or how's the work to get along? 2. Miyāṅ, tu ham se na rakh kuchh gubār Holī meṅ! Ki rūṭhe milte haiṅ āpas meṅ yār Holī meṅ . Nazīr. Dear Sir! in festive Holī bear not malice aught 'gainst me! In Holi hearts estranged before should now united be. rūṭhā-rūṭhī ; Sant. reṭhā riṭhī , n. f. Misunderstanding; mutual coolness.
adj. Touchy; testy; hasty; choleric.
adj. Quarrelsome, irritable, testy, sensitive; — s.f. A species of sensitive plant, Mimosa natans.
more: 1. adj. Quarrel some. 2. s. f. The name of a species of sensitive plant. (Mimosa natans).

def: v.n. = rūṭhnā, q.v.

ety: for A. رطوبة, inf. n. of رطب 'to be moist,' &c.
def: s.f. Moisture, humidity, dampness, juiciness, sap, juice; saliva; freshness (pl. rut̤ūbāt).
more: n. f. رطب being moist. 1. Moisture; humidity; damp ( tarī , 1). 2. Sap; juice. 3. Spit; saliva.
(from رطب) s. f. 1. Humidity, moisture. 2. Freshness. (Plur. رطوبات)

def: (caus. of ṛonā), v.t (dialec.) To cause to weep, make (one) cry, &c. ( = rulānā, q.v.).
s.f. (dialec.) = rūhat, q.v. s.f. Brightness, freshness, cheerfulness; beauty; — regard, attention to the wishes (of).
more: s. f. (dakh.) 1. Beauty, symmetry: see رونق raunaḳ. 2. Regard, attention to the wishes: see پاس خاطر under پاس or see ملاحظه.

def: s.f. Dream, vision.

ety: for A. رؤية, inf. n. of رأي 'to see'
def: s.f. 'Seeing'; sight, vision; view; countenance, appearance, shape; aspect (of the planets, &c.); consideration, regard.
more: n. f. رای seeing. 1. Aspect; view. 2. Countenance ( pās , 3).
prop. rūyat (from رأي Heb. To see) s. f. 1. Shape, appearance. 2. Seeing, regard, consideration. 3. Aspect of the planets.

def: adj. See riẕāla, and rizāl.
insep. prefix, With, together with, along with; having, possessing, accompanied by, &c. (it is combined with nouns to form adjectives and adverbs).

ety: Prk. सरिअओ or सलिअओ; S. सट्टश+कः
def: adj. suffix, (f. -ī), Like, resembling, as (it is annexed to the accusatival base of adjectives, substantives, and participles, and to the formative or oblique base of the personal pronouns, and forms adjectives; e.g. kālā-sā, 'black-like,' 'blackish'; laṛkā-sā, 'boy-like,' 'boyish'; tujh-sā (rarely tu-sā), 'like thee'; terā-sā, 'like thine').
more: adj. S. सदृश P. ساں Eng. ish . An affix, Like; resembling; so; as kālā sā blackish, tumsā like you.

1. adj. (Pers. سان or Sans. समान or सदृश ) Like, resembling (most commonly by way of adjunct like the English) ish; so کالاسا kālā-sā, Blackish. 2. (for s. शः ) An adjunct the meaning of which is at times scarcely perceptible, though often it seems to give intensity to the preceding word; as بـہت سا bahut-sā, Much, many, very much. 3. (or ساي) part. in compos. (from سودن or ساييدن) Rubbing, wearing, polishing, anointing, drawing.

def: adv. suffix (f. -ī), Very, intensely, &c. (it is annexed to adjectives, and to the relative, interrogative, and indefinite pronouns; and forms adjectives with an intensified signification; e.g. kālā-sā, 'very black'; bahut-sā, 'very much or many,' 'a great deal of,' 'a great many'; thorā-sā, 'very little'; kaun-sā, 'which particular one' (out of a number)?; koʼī-sā, 'any one whatever'; kućh-sā, 'somewhat').

ety: rt. of sūdan; = Zend śā = S. शा (शो, श्यति)
def: part. Rubbing, wearing; polishing; anointing; drawing (used as last member of compounds).
s.f. A name of Durgā.
s.m. A measure of five pints (art̤āl) and a third (used for measuring corn, &c.).
more: s. A measure consisting of four mudd, each mudd being equal to one and one third rat̤l.

def: s.m. corr. of shāh, q.v. s.m. King, prince, monarch, &c.; the king (at chess or cards); a title assumed by faqīrs; — adj. (in comp.) Royal, noble, great, excellent, principal; the longest, largest, or best (of its kind), &c.
subs: s.m. A kind of plum; a prune, Prunus cerabus
adj. Royal, noble, generous; — s.m. A royal falcon

s.m. The boy (of the same age, height, and figure as the bridegroom, and similarly dressed and decorated) who accompanies the bridegroom to the bride's house on the night of the barāt; — the groom or attendant who rides behind a great man (also shah-bālā)
s.m. 'Royal oak'; the oak; the chesnut
s.m. Chief port or harbour; (local) custom-house; — a harbour-master; the receiver-general of duties or tribute
(and shah-par), s.m. The largest or strongest feather (in a bird's wing)
(and shah-tīr), s.m. The large beam (of a house); a beam
s.m. 'The monarch of the East,' the sun
s.f. A kind of sweetmeat used on great occasions
s.m. A village on a river (in front of a king's palace); — the highway
s.m. 'Old man of the sea'; one of the seven demons held in awe by Moḥammadan women
s.f. 'King's road,' the highway, main road; a principal street
(and shah-zādagī), s.f. The state, or the youth, of a prince; nobility (syn. amīrī); the minority of a prince; — youthful folly; foolish pride
(and shah-zāda), s.m. 'King's son,' the prince-royal; a prince; (in the language of women) a dear son
s.f. A princess; a queen; — the state of prince or princess
(and shah-sawār), s.m. A good rider, an expert horseman
adj. Pleasant-paced, having easy paces (a horse); — s.m. The royal pace, name of a particular pace of a horse
s.m. 'King of the firmament,' the sun
'King of heroes,' a title of the Khalīfa ʻAlī
s.m. 'Monarch of the East,' the sun
s.f. A celebrated epic poem, containing a history of the kings of Persia (written by Firdausī about the year of Christ 1000)

(and shah-nishīn), s.m. The king's seat (i.e. a gallery or balcony projecting from the palace, where the king shows himself to his people); a balcony, gallery; the principal seat in an assembly, a president's seat
(and shah-wār), adj. Royal, princely, noble; like a king; fit for a king, befitting a king; — s.m. A fine royal pearl.

ety: for A. سعادة, inf. n. of سعد 'to be prosperous,' &c.
def: s.f. Prosperity, good fortune, happiness, felicity
more: n. f. سعد was prosperous. Auspiciousness; felicity ( bakhtāorī , 1). saādat-mand , adj. Dutiful; obedient. saādat-mandī , n. f. Dutifulness; obedience.
(from سعد) s. f. Felicity, happiness, good fortune. سعادت مند saʼāﻌdat-mand, adj. Fortunate, prosperous, happy. سعادت مندي saʼāﻌdat-mand-ī, s. f. Prosperity.
subs: adj. Prosperous, fortunate, happy, blessed; dutiful, obedient

def: s.f. Time; at hour; a short time, a little while; a minute; a moment; — a clock, a watch; — sāʻat-sāz, s.m. Watch-maker ( = ghaṛī sāz).
more: n. f. 1. A moment; minute ( pal ). 1. Woh to ghaṛī, sāat kā hai . He may die at any moment. 2. Khoṭī shāat na āve ! Wom. May evil hour ne'er come! 2. Hour; watch; time.
s. f. A moment, minute, hour. ساعت ساز sāʼaﻌt-sāz, Clockmaker.
s. f. A clock or watch.

def: adj. & s.m. All, entire, whole, total; every; any; — all people, everybody; the whole (the oblique pl. is generally sabhoṅ)
more: adj. S. सर्व Old H. sarab ; Pr. savvo . See sārā . 1. All; every ( sārā ). 2. Total; integral; aggregate. Rām nām ke kārne sab dhan ḍālā khoë, Mūrakh jāne gir paṛā, din din dūnā hoë . Prov. In Rām's name all his wealth the needy he supplied. The fool esteems it lost, each day it's multiplied. 3. Uncut; undivided; unbroken; one. 4. National; public. sab , n. m. pl. sabhoṅ . The whole; an amount. Sab ne manā kīyā, magar ek na mānā. sab hāl , Full particulars; the whole case. sab dunyā, sab log , n. m. The public; all men; all mankind; the world. sab samet , All together. sab se achchhā , adj. Best; prime. sab kā sab , adv. Wholly; altogether; in the gross. sab kuchh ; Sant. sanāmā , adv. Every thing; all. sab koī, sab , n. m. Every body; every one. sab kahīṅ, sab jagah ; Sant. sanām ṭhen , adv. Everywhere. sab ke dekhte, robrū yā sāmne , adv. Before everybody; in public; in the eyes of all…

adj. All, every, the whole, total. (In the inflection of the plural, this word generally becomes سبهون sabhoṅ ). سب مصري کي هين ڐليان sab miṣrī kī haiṅ ḍaliyāṅ (All are pieces of sugar) is applied to denote equality or similitude of state, form, &c. سب کچهہ sab kuchh, Every thing, all. سب کوٴي sab koʼī, Every body, every one, all. سبکهوا sab-khauwā, Omnivorous, one who eats all kinds of food without exception.
subs: vulg. sab-gyān, adj. & s.m. All-knowing, omniscient; — all knowledge, knowledge of all
s.m. The whole case, all the facts, full particulars
adv. All together, in all, in the total
adj. (f. -ī), Of or pertaining to all, general, common, universal, public
(f. sab-kī-sab), s.m. & adv. The whole (of a thing), all of it together; altogether, completely
s.m. pl. & adv. All without exception (of a number of objects), the whole of them together; completely
adj. & s.m. Omnivorous; — one who eats everything and anything
s.m. The whole world, all people, everybody.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀩𑁆𑀩 (sabba), 𑀲𑀯𑁆𑀯 (savva), from Ashokan Prakrit 𑀲𑀯𑁆𑀯 (savva), 𑀲𑀭𑁆𑀯 (sarva), from Sanskrit सर्व (sárva), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *sárwas, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *sárwas, from Proto-Indo-European *solh₂-wó-s, from *solh₂- (“whole”). Doublet of ہر. Cognate with Kamkata-viri suv, Persian هر (har).

def: adj. Seven
more: (Heb. ) adj. Seven. سبع سياره sabʼāﻌ saiyāra, The seven planets. ¶ subﻌ, A seventh part.

def: s.m. = śav, q.v.; — 2˚ adj. corr. of sab, q.v. s.m. Dead body, corpse.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شب (šab). Cognate of Sanskrit क्षप् (kṣap).

ety: v.n. fr. صعب 'to be difficult, or hard,' &c.
def: adj. Difficult, hard, arduous, troublesome, hard to be borne or endured, disagreeable, distressing; — refractory, untractable, disobedient, stubborn, perverse.
more: adj. Hard, difficult, arduous, rough, troublesome, disagreeable, disobedient, stubborn, perverse.
wety: Ultimately from Arabic صَعْب (ṣaʕb).

def: prop. n. Name of a country in Arabia Felix, Sheba (where Bilqīs, the queen who visited Solomon, is supposed to have reigned).
more: n. prop. A country in Arabia Felix, where they suppose Bilḳīs, the queen who visited Solomon, to have reigned.

ety: inf. n. of صَبَا, rt. صبو 'to play or sport,' &c.
def: s.f. (orig.) 'The east wind, or an easterly wind'; a gentle and pleasant breeze; the morning breeze; the zephyr.
more: n. f. The morning breeze; a gentle breeze; zephyr. 1. Kal jo the yāṅ gul khile, āj unkī ek ek pankhṛī, Sāmne mere uṛā-kar le gaī bād-i-sabā . Life. Nazīr. But yesterday these flowers did bloom, today their petals all Before my eyes the breeze hath swept away beyond recal! 2. Jo chaman meṅ guzre tū, aë sabā, to yehi kahiye bulbul-i-zār se, Ki khizāṅ ke din bhī haiṅ sāmne, na lagānā dil ko bahār se! If Zephyr, you the garden sweep to the bird of song just say, The Autumn's nigh, to flowers of Spring give not thy heart away!
s. f. A gentle breeze, an easterly breeze, a morning breeze.
s. (dakh.) Morning, morrow: see صباح ṣabāḥ.

def: s.f. corr. of sabhā, q.v. s.f. Assembly, meeting, company; council; a sitting of the king in council; a judicial court; a levee; hall of audience; a gaming-house; a much-frequented place; — place, house
subs: s.m. President or chairman of an assembly; — keeper of a gaming-house
(˚bhā+ad˚), s.m. President or chief of an assembly
s.m. One of a company, a member of an assembly, or society, or council, an assistant at an assembly or meeting; (in law an assessor; an arbitrator
(-kī), To convene or to hold a meeting

ety: v.n. fr. سبّ; ii سبّب 'to appoint or prepare a means or canse'
def: s.m. (orig. 'a rope,' 'a means by which one reaches'; hence) Means of access (to) or attainment (of), means; cause; reason, motive; instrument; connexion of relationship, relationship, affinity; — postpn. On account (of, -ke), by reason (of); by means (of).
more: n. m. 1. Cause; ground (باعث 1). 2. Means; instrument. ṣabab-i-avval . The first or primary cause; God. sabab-i-baīd . The ultimate cause. sabab-i-qarīb , A proximate or immediate cause. sabab-i-nālish , Cause of complaint; ground of action. ba-sabab ; H. kāran se , adv. By reason of; because of; in consequence of; on account of; in pursuance of ( duāre , and zariye se ). be-sabab , adj. 1. Groundless; without foundation. 2. Causeless; wanton. be-sabab , adv. 1. Causelessly; for nothing. 2. Wantonly.
(Heb. from سبّب To cause, or Heb. To turn, q. d. A turn of affairs) s. m. 1. Cause, reason, motive. 2. Relationship, affinity. 3. Instrument.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَبَب (sabab), from Arabic سَبَب (sabab).

def: s.f. Causation; cause, motive.
adj. = sāvadhān, q.v. adj. Attentive, heedful, careful, cautious, circumspect; prudent; diligent
more: adj. Attentive, cautious, with attention. See ساودهان sāvadhān.
subs: (-se), To be careful or mindful (of), to beware (of)
v.t. To make (one) careful or cautious, to put (one) on (his) guard, to warn; — v.n. To take care, exercise caution, to be careful or cautious

def: s.f. Daybreak, dawn, early morning, morning ( = ṣubḥ); — name of a bird
more: s. f. The morning, dawn of day. صباح الخير ṣabāḥu-l-ḵẖair, 1. Morning salutation, good morning! 2. Name of a bird.
subs: (morning salutation), Good morning! — ʻalaʼṣ-ṣabāḥ, adv. Before or at dawn, early in the morning.

ety: for A. صباحة, inf. n. of صبح 'to be beautiful,' &c.
def: s.f. Beauty, comeliness, gracefulness, elegance; brightness of face, fairness of complexion.
more: n. f. Opp. of malāhat . Fairness of complexion.

def: adj. Of the gentle breeze, &c.
adj. Of or relating to a gentle breeze, &c.
s.m. Lesson, lecture, reading
more: n. m. سبق preceded. A lesson; reading. Sabaq aur tabaq dono maujūd haiṅ . Prov. Learning and food are both before you. sabaq paṛhnā , v. n. To read a lesson. sabaq paṛhānā, yā denā , To teach; give a lesson. sabaq lenā , v. n. To take a lesson.
(from سبق Chald. To precede, to leave behind) s. m. A lesson, lecture, reading.
subs: To read or study a lesson
sabaq denā (-ko), To give a lesson (to), to teach
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سبق (sabaq), from Arabic سَبَق (sabaq).

def: s.m. corr. of شبکه shabaka, q.v. s.m. A net; lattice; trellis; grating; a reticulated veil, &c. (syn. jāl, jālī).
s.f. = sabqat, q.v. s.f. Going or getting before, taking the lead, preceding, outstripping, surpassing, excelling, precedence, excellence, superiority; aggression
subs: (-par), To go before, to precede, to advance; to get before, to outstrip, surpass; to outvie; to be beforehand (with)
(-par or -se), To bear away the palm (from), to excel, to outstrip, to surpass.

def: s.m.(?) An iron crow or lever; an instrument used by burglars to break into houses; — a small anvil ( = sābar).
s.m. Falling rain (before it reaches the ground); — an ear of corn; — a disease in the eye resembling a film with red veins, a cataract; blood-shot eye; blear-eye.
adj. (f. -ā), Strong, powerful, forcible, energetic.
more: adj. Strong; powerful; forcible. Sabaī sahāyak sabal ke, koū na nibal sahāë, Pavan jagāvat āg ko, dīpak det bujhāë . Supporters of the strong all are, the weak to back no one, The fire fanned by the wind's ablaze, the light wick is out blown.
n. m. Inflammation of the eye; blood-shot or blear eye.
s. m. 1. A disorder of the eyes, a cataract or the being bloodshot or bleareyed. 2. An ear of corn. 3. Rain (especially, whilst falling from the clouds).

ety: sabal, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Strength, powerfulness, power, force, energy.
s. f. Power, force, strength.
s. (dakh. from صباح) The morn, the morrow; in the morning.
s.m. A large species of stag, a kind of elk; — leather of elk's (or any kind of deer's) hide, chamois, wash-leather.
more: n. m. S. शम्बर. 1. An elk; a species of large stag. 2. Imitation Chamois leather. 3. A burglar's instrument for breaking into houses.

s. m. 1. An elk. 2. A leather of elkʼs or any kind of deerʼs hide for bedding. سابرمنتر sābar-mantr ( शावरमंत्र from शवर A low Hindū cast, and मंत्र A spell) s. m. A spell composed in colloquial language.

def: adj. & s.m. Belonging to a savage or barbarian, barbarous; — a barbarian, a low man
subs: s.m. A mantra composed in a barbarous or colloquial language (such as one of the Prākrits).

def: s.m. = sābal, q.v. s.m.(?) An iron crow or lever; an instrument used by burglars to break into houses; — a small anvil ( = sābar).
v. n. (dakh.) To be patient.
s.m. A hare; rabbit (syn. sasā).
more: s. m. A hare, a rabbit.

def: s.f. corr. of shābāshī, q.v. s.f. Praise, applause, plaudits
1˚, s.m. corr. of sahwāsī, q.v.; — 2˚, s.f. corr. of shābāshī, q.v.
= H سابستي साबस्ती sābastī, s.f. corr. of sahavasatī, q.v.
= H سابست साबस्त sābast, s.f. corr. of sahavasatī, q.v.
n. m. ثبت subsisted. 1. Stability; permanence; permanency. Dunyā be-sabāt hai . Prov. This is a transitory world. 2. Firmness; resolution; constancy.
more: s. m. Permanency, stability, firmness, resolution, constancy.

def: s.f. = saut, q.v. (cf. sawat). s.f. A co-wife, contemporary wife, rival wife (the first wife is called byāhtā; each wife is saut to the other); a rival; pair, twins (e.g. two mangoes, &c. that grow together)
subs: s.m. A spiteful or malicious person, a rival.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَبْت (sabt), from Arabic اَلسَّبْت (as-sabt).

def: s.m. Continuance, subsistency, durability, permanence, stability, endurance; constancy, firmness, steadiness, steadfastness, fixedness; resolution, determination; soundness, validity.
adj. = sautiyā, q.v. adj. Of or belonging or relating to a co-wife (saut)
subs: or sautiyā-dāh, or sautiyā-ḍāh, s.f. Rivalry, or jealousy, or malice of a contemporary wife; malicious or malignant rivalry.

def: adj. corr. of 1˚ sawayā; 2˚ savya, qq.v. adj. With a quarter more, &c. ( = sawāyā, q.v.); — s.m. A quarter more; a weight of 1 ¼ ser; the multiplication table of 1 ¼ times; a kind of verse or measure (consisting of four lines, each of 16+15 = 31 instants, the last foot being a trochee); a song in this measure; a singer of the sawaiyā
subs: s.m. A species of metre ( = sawaiyā).
wety: Named after Rani Sewi, a Hindu woman who ruled Balochistan before the 7th century.

def: s.m. = sābal, q.v. s.m.(?) An iron crow or lever; an instrument used by burglars to break into houses; — a small anvil ( = sābar).

def: s.f. Podex, anus.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَبْت (sabt), from Arabic اَلسَّبْت (as-sabt).

def: s.m. = شبد śabd, q.v.
more: s. m. 1. Sound in general. 2. A sound, a word. 3. (in grammar) A declinable word, as a noun, pronoun, &c. 4. A song (among Nānak-panthīs ).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَبَد (sabad).

def: s.m. Sound, noise; voice; grammar; a word; a name, term; (in Gram.) a declinable word (as a noun, pronoun, &c.) without its termination; — verbal communication; (among Nānak-panthīs), a song, a hymu
more: n. m. 1. Sound; voice. 2. A word; name; term. 3. ( Gram .) A declinable noun or pronoun. 4. ( Nānak-panthī ) A hymn. shabd-ārth ; P. lafzī māne , The literal meaning or acceptation; the primary meaning. shabdā shabd , adv. Verbatim ; word for word. shabd Brahm , n. m. A Ved. shabd-bedhī , n. m. lit . piercing by sound. One skilled in shooting an unseen object from which a sound is heard. shabd-chor ; A. sāriq , n. m. A plagiarist. shabd-chorī ; A. sarqah-i-ma/?/mūn , n. f. Plagiary. shabd-sādhan , n. m. Etymology. shabd shāstr , n. m. The science of grammatical inflection; etymology; rules of grammar. shabd-kār , adj. Sonorous; sounding. shabd k. , v. a To sound; blow (a horn). shabd-kosh , n. m. A dictionary; vocabulary. shabd-yonī , n. f. A radical word.

s. m. Sound, word, voice. See سبد sabd. شبد شاستر शब्दशास्त्र shabd-shāstr, Etymology, the science of grammatical inflection. شبد بيدهي shabd-bedhī ( शब्दवेधी Piercing by sound) One who has the skill of shooting an arrow, &c. upon an unseen person, on hearing a sound made by him: (an arrow) that pierces by sound, or that hits what is merely heard.
subs: (˚da+ar˚), s.m. The sense or meaning of a word; the literal meaning or acceptation
s.m. lit. 'Sound-piercer'; an epithet of Arjun; — a particular fabulous arrow (which pierces an object the sound of which only is heard; — cf. śabd-vedhī)
s.m. 'The science of words,' rules of grammar; the science of grammatical inflexion, etymology; grammar; philology
adj. Making noise or sound, sounding, sonorous
v.n. To make a noise or sound (with, -se), to sound, blow
(˚da+ut˚), s.f. The origin or the production of words

vulg. śabd-bedhī, adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), 'Sound-piercing,' piercing (an invisible object) the sound (of which only is heard; e.g. sabd-bedhī tīr); — an archer who can pierce an invisible object the sound of which only is heard
s.f. 'Word-source'; the source or origin of a word, a radical word, a root.

śabd śobhā sakhī tirī lobhā
tū sakhī mujh se māvrā kaisī

The King's court goes to Hell. sabhā-patī ; P. mīr-majlis; sadar-nashīṅ , n. m. A president; chairman. sabhā-sad , n. m. 1. A member of an assembly or council. 2. An arbitrator; assessor. sabhā sammat . A ward of a council. sabhā k. , v. a. To convene or hold a meeting. rāj-sabhā ; P. darbār , n. m. 1. A royal court. 2. The chief court of justice, at which the king or his representative presided. grām-sabhā ; Sant. kulhī duṛup , ( lit . to sit in the street) n. m. A village court composed of the principal inhabitants with the head man presiding.
s. f. An assembly, meeting, company.

(˚bhā+ad˚), s.m. President or chief of an assembly
s.m. One of a company, a member of an assembly, or society, or council, an assistant at an assembly or meeting; (in law an assessor; an arbitrator
(-kī), To convene or to hold a meeting
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit सभा (sabhā).

s.m. = S سبهيتا सभ्यता sabhyatā, s.f. Refinement in manners, good breeding, politeness.

more: adj. Beautiful. s. A fine complexion.

def: adj. & s.m. Preceding, foregoing, former, prior, past, ancient; — past time, former times; — adv. = sābiq-meṅ, q.v.
more: adj. سبق he preceded him. Former; prior; past; foregoing; preceding. sābiq-ul-zikr . Before-mentioned. sābiq dastūr , As before; as usual. sābiq meṅ , adv. Of yore; heretofore; in times past ( pahle ).

(part. act. of سبق Chald. To outstrip, to pass by) adj. Former, preceding, anteriour, foregoing. s. Past time, former times. adv. (or سابق مين sābiḳ meṅ ) Formerly, of yore, heretofore, ago, beforetime.
subs: adv. According to former practice, as before, as usual; — sābiq-meṅ, adv. Formerly, of yore, in times past, heretofore.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سابق (sābiq), from Arabic سَابِق (sābiq).

ety: for A. سابقة sābiqat, fem. of sābiq
def: adj. Preceding, &c. ( = sābiq, q.v.); — s.f. The past; ancient right or custom; — pre-excellence, pre-eminence, priority, precedence, superiority; — long-standing acquaintance, friendship, intimacy, close intercourse, correspondence; business (with), transaction, dealings
more: n. m. 1. Old acquaintance or friendship. 2. Correspondence; intercourse ( rābitā , 1.). Khudā aisoṅ se sābiqā na ḍāle! 3. A transaction ( muāmlah ). sābiqa paṛnā , v. n. 1. To have to do deal with. 2. To come in contact with; get acquainted with.
(from سبق) 1. adj. Preceding, past, ancient. 2. s. m. Friendship, intimacy.
subs: (-se), To become acquainted (with), to be brought into close intercourse (with), to have to deal (with)
(-se), To cast (one's, -kā) lot (with), to bring (one) into close connection (with).

def: adv. Before, &c. ( = sābiq-meṅ); as formerly, as before.

s.f. Way, road, path; course, manner of proceeding; manner, mode; means of access, means, instrument; water or sherbet given to thirsty travellers during the festival of the Moharram; water or other drink given as a pious duty; a shed, &c. in which water is kept for thirsty travellers
more: n. f. سبل See tarkīb . 1. Course; way; process; manner of proceeding. 2. Instrument; means. 3. Mode; manner. 4. A road; path. 5. Water or other drinks distributed along the road to the people at the festival of Moharram, etc. Bole koi nek/?/ kamāī-vālā, pilā deṅ sabīl! Bhistīs' cry. Let some honest earner for water pay, in God's name all shall drink. 6. Sheds in which such liquids are placed for distribution. sabīl zar-i-adāë bāqī , n. f. Source of payment. sabīl karnā , v. a. To obtain money by borrowing or begging; raise the wind; fly a kite. sabīl lagānā yā pilānā , v. a. To give a drink of water gratis . fī sabīl allah k. , v. a. To give in God's name; to give alms.
(Heb. and Ch. ) s. f. 1. A road, way, path, mode, manner. 2. Water distributed to thirsty travellers during the ten first days of muḥarram.
subs: The way, or cause, of God or of religion, the means of advancement in God's favour; — sabīl pilānā, v.t. To give (one) water or sherbet, &c. to drink as a pious duty, to give water, &c. to drink gratis
sabīl lagānā, To keep a shed for the supplying thirty travellers, &c. with water during the month of Moharram; to give water gratis to travellers, &c. ( = sabīl pilānā)
v.t. To prepare, arrange; to get together; to obtain money by begging or borrowing
(-ě, or -ke), adv. By way (of), by means (of), by, through the medium (of)
adv. In the path of God, for the sake of God, for religious or sacred uses, as a religious or pious duty.

adj. Continuing, subsisting, lasting, enduring, remaining; remaining fixed or stationary, standing, resting; permanent, constant, firm, steady, steadfast, stable, fixed, fast, settled, established, confirmed, proved, ascertained; sound, valid, substantial, real, sure, certain, true, right, correct, just, proper
subs: (-meṅ, or -par), To continue firm (in); to adhere or stick (to); to be obstinate (in respect of)

adj. Firm, immovable, stable, steady, steadfast; permanent, constant; resolute, determined; persevering; — s̤ābit-qadam karnā (with acc. -ko of person, and loc. -par of thing), To render or make (one) firm, steady, or constant, &c. (in, or in respect of)
(-ko), To confirm (in, -par), to establish, render durable; to prove, verify, bring home (to, -par), substantiate
v.n. To be confirmed, be established, be proved, &c.

def: adj. ثبت subsisted. 1. Sound; entire ( pūrā ). 2. Firm; stable; constant; fixed (a star). 3. Proved; established; substantiated; convicted. sābit-qadam , adj. 1. Stable; immoveable; steady; constant. 2. Firm; faithful; staunch; steadfast. 3. Resolute; determined; unflinching. 4. Persevering; unintermitting. Sābīt-qadam ko sab jag ṭhāoṅ . Prov. Constant your mind, in all the world a footing find. sābit k . v. a. To prove; support the truth of; make good (a charge). sābit honā , v. n. To be proved; established.
more: (from ثبوت) adj. Firm, constant, durable, stable, proved, ascertained, sure, valid, confirmed, established, fixed; a fixed star. ثابت رهنا s̤ābit rahnā, To adhere to a person. ثابت قدم s̤ābit-ḳadam, adj. Immovable, stable, resolute, permanent, constant. ثابت کرنا s̤ābit karnā, To confirm, establish, render durable, verify, prove (by witness). ثابت هونا s̤ābit honā, To be proved or confirmed.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ثابت (sābit), from Arabic ثَابِت (ṯābit), active participle of ثَبَتَ (ṯabata, “to be firm, to be fixed, to persist”).

(from سبق) s. f. Getting before, outstripping, surpassing, preceding, excellence, aggression. سبقت کرنا sabḳat karnā, To get before, to outstrip or precede, to surpass, to advance.
subs: (-par), To go before, to precede, to advance; to get before, to outstrip, surpass; to outvie; to be beforehand (with)

ety: inf. n. of صبر 'to be patient,' &c.
def: s.m.f. Patience, self-restraint, endurance, patient suffering, resignation
more: n. m. 1. Patience. 1. Sabar kā ajar Khudā degā . Prov. Saying. God will requite the patient heart. 2. Terī jān par sabar kīye baiṭhī hūṅ! Wom. I patient wait to see thee die. 3. Sabr kā phal mīṭhā hai . Prov. The fruit of patience īs sweet. 4. Sabr kī dād Khudā ke hāth . Wom. The reward of patience is in God's hand. 2. Sufferance ( bardāsht , 1). Nibar ko zabar, zabar ko sabar . Prov. The strong may throw the weak, but the weak man's sighs will throw the strong. 3. Submission; resignation ( tavakkul ). sabarpaṛnā , v. n. To reap evil; to be scourged; to be under a curse. sabar sameṭnā, yā lenā , v. a. 1. To provoke Divine wrath. 2. ( tohmat lagānā ) To accuse falsely; to slander. sabar k. , v. a. 1. To wait; have patience. 2. To endure; bear up or with; bear meekly. Musibat meṅ sabar karnā javāṅmardoṅ kā kām hai . To bear adversity with patience is the part of a brave man. 3. To content …
s. f. Patience, endurance. صبرکرنا ṣabr karnā, To wait, to have patience, to endure. ¶ ṣabir, A species of aloes.
subs: (-par), The patient suffering (of one oppressed) to be visited (on the oppressor, e.g. merā ṣabr tujh-par paṛegā, 'my patient suffering will be visited on thee'); to fall into calamity, to fall under a curse
(-kā), To store up calamity or evil (for oneself through wrong-doing), to reap the reward of evil, to provoke the visitation of Heaven; — to accuse falsely, to slander

v.n. To exercise self-restraint, to practise patience, to be patient, to wait; to endure, to bear up patiently (under or on, -meṅ or -par, or against, -se); to abstain or refrain (from, -se); to content oneself (with)
v.n. To be involved in calamity, &c. (see ṣabr paṛnā, and ṣabr sameṭnā)
(-ko), To have or possess patience; to be contented or satisfied
adj. Impatient; restless, fidgety; hard to satisfy, avaricious, greedy
s.m. or be-ṣabrī, s.f. Impatience; restlessness; greediness, avariciousness, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صَبْر (sabr), borrowed from Arabic صَبْر (ṣabr). Compare Bengali সবর (śobor).

def: adj. Light (not heavy); light-footed, expeditious, active, nimble; light, frivolous, trivial, trifling; shallow; futile; unsteady; undignified, degrading, debased; delicate, slim

(prop. sabuk ) adj. Light (either in weight or character), debased, unsteady, trivial, trifling, frivolous, futile. سبکبار subuk-bār, adj. Swiftness of foot. سبک پرواز subuk-parwāz, Light or swift in flight. سبک خيز subuk-ḵẖez, Quickly or swiftly rising, swift. سبک دوش subuk-dosh, Lightly laden. سبکرو subuk-rau, Lightly or easily going, light-paced. سبک روحي subuk-rauḥ-ī, s. f. Lightness of blowing: subuk-rūḥ-ī, s. f. Cheerfulness, gayety, lightness or ease of mind. سبک روٴي subuk-rau-ī, s. f. Swiftness, celerity. سبک سير subuk-sair, adj. Quick, swift, light-footed.

adj. Light or swift in flight, light-winged
adj. Rising quickly or swiftly, alert, brisk, quick, active, vigilant
adj. Lightly laden, unencumbered; relieved, free from care or anxiety, light-hearted
adj. Going lightly or easily, light-paced, travelling quickly, quick, swift, fleet
s.f. Cheerfulness, gaiety, lightness or ease of mind

What you say is true, old woman! what you say is true! ☞ The story goes that some men having robbed an old woman bound her to a stretcher and so bore her through the streets, replying, to her cries that she was plundered, in the words of the Proverb. Thus the people were deluded into the idea that the procession was one of the mimic representations commonly exhibited in India. 4. Sāṅch kahe so mārā jāë, jhūṅṭh kahe dulārā jāë . Bhoj. Prov. sach bolnā yā kahnā, sat kahnā , v. n. To speak truly. 1. Sach bol, pūrā tol . Prov. Speak the truth, and give just weight. 2. Sach bolnā aur sukhī rahnā . Prov. more: called صادمـﮩمله and صادغيرمنقوطه the 14th letter of the Arabic alphabet, and 20th in Hindustāni, does not properly occur in the Persian language. In abjad , q. v. it stands for 90. As an abbreviation (ص) it is used for صحيح q. v. When Persian words containing che are introduced into Arabic, the چ is changed into ص as صين for چين China. suād k. , v. a. 1. To approve; sign; signify the correctness of; check; make a mark. 2. To grant; sanction.

the fourteenth letter of the Arabic alphabet, does not properly occur in the Persian language; but, in a few words it has, in modern times, been substituted for the original س to distinguish them from others of a different signification; as, in صد, originally سد One hundred; and in شصت originally شست Sixty; to prevent their being confounded with سد A wall, and شست An aim. It has no corresponding letter in the Sanskrit alphabet. In Persia and Hindūstān, its pronunciation is hardly distinguishable from that of س; but, among the Arabs, its sound is stronger than the last and with a mixture of w. In the notation of numbers by the letters of the Arabic alphabet it represents 90. As an abbreviation it is used for صفر the name of the second month in the Arabian calendar. It is one of the radical letters. Some Arabic words are written indifferently with ص and س; as صراط, or سراط A road. Sometime…

s.f. Echo; sound, noise; voice, tone, cry, call; (among Khattrīs, by whom it is corr. pron. saddā) an invitation (to a marriage ceremony, or a feast)
more: n. f. 1. Sound; tone; voice; echo (P. āvāz , 2, 4, 5.). sad'dā , (Khattris), n. m. An invitation to join in a marriage ceremony ( bulāvā ). Anī tāyā jī to sadde dene gaë haiṅ. sadā lagānā , v. a. To cry as mendicants; to beg. tarkārī-vāloṅ kī sadā yā āvāzeṅ . Street cries. faqīroṅ kī sadā, sadā . Mendicants' cry.
s. f. Sound, tone, echo, voice, noise.
subs: (corr. saddā) denā (-ko), To call or invite (to a marriage ceremony, &c.)
v.n. To cry, call out (as vendors of wares, mendicants, &c.); to beg.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صدا (sadā), from Arabic صَدًى (ṣadan).

def: s.m. The herb rue.

Mah. n. m. Alms or food distributed daily to the poor, or to travellers, etc. Unke yahāṅ sadā-barat baṭtā hai! sadā-bahār , adj. ( Bot .) Evergreen; perennial; sempervirens . sadā-phal , adj. Ever-bearing fruit (a tree). sadā-phal , n. m. A tree which bears fruit every year, esp. the sweet lime, the cocoanut, the glomerous fig, the jack, the bel , q. v. sadā-chār , n. m. Everyday religious rites; the daily practices of the Hindūs. Sadā-chār, jap, yog, birāgā, Sa-bhai bibek kaṭak sab bhāgā . Rāmāyan. sadā-sukhī . adj. Ever happy.
more: s. m. Alms, or food distributed daily to the poor, travellers, &c.

def: = S سداتما सदात्मा sadātmā, See s.v. sat, 'real,' &c.
s.f. The mother-of-pearl shell; mother-of-pearl; — an oyster-shell; a shell; — a pearl.
more: n. f. A shell; mother of pearl.
s. f. A shell, a pearl, mother of pearl.

def: s.f. Plainness, absence of ornament; artlessness, simplicity, openness, frankness, sincerity, purity.
more: n. f. 1. Plainness; want of ornament. 2. ( sādah-dilī ) Simplicity; openness; frankness; artlessness; sincerity. 1. Do bosoṅ kī jo un se ijāzat milī hameṅ, Aur ham ne le līye kaī zyādah hazār se, Allah re sādgī! ki na samjhe woh kuchh hisāb, Kahne lage na lījye zyādah qarār se! To take two kisses I permission had of her, But I took o'er a thousand kisses I aver; God bless her innocence! no arithmetic she knew, But said 'pray dont take more than my compact with you!' 2. Is /?/ādgī pe kaun na mar jāë, e Khudā! Larṭe haiṅ, aur hāth meṅ talvār bhī nahīṅ! Gālib. O God! who would not give his life for such simplicity, No arms nor armour she has got and yet she fights with me!
(from ساده) s. f. 1. Plainness, want of ornament. 2. Artlessness, simplicity, openness, sincerity, frankness.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سادگی.

def: n. m. Simplicity ( sādgī , 2.). Na jiske choḷī na jiske kaṅghī, na jisko karnā singār āve, Abhī to sādā-pan hī gazab hai, jidhar ko dekhe na vār āve . Nor hair turned up as yet, nor comb, nor ornamental aid, Her simple garb e'en now such ruin makes, all bleeding laid!

ety: sadā, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. tā+ikā
def: s.f. Perpetuity, continuousness.
s.m. The two fortunate planets, Jupiter and Venus (or Venus and Mercury).
more: s. dual, Two fortunate planets, Jupiter and Venus.

def: adj. (Braj.) = sa-deśī, q.v. adj. Of or belonging to one's own, or to the same, country or locality; — sva-deśī, s.m. A fellow-countryman.
adv. See s.v. sadā.
s.m. Indian spikenard; leaves of the Laurus cassia?

ety: for A. صداقة, v.n. fr. صدق 'to speak truth,' &c.
def: s.f. True or sincere friendship; love, affection; friendliness; sincerity, candour; loyalty, fidelity; truth.
more: n. f. صدق told the truth. See sachchā-pan .
(from صدق Heb. and Ch. Was just, true, sincere) s. f. 1. Sincerity, candour. 2. Friendship. 3. Truth.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صَدَاقَت (sadāqat), from Arabic صَدَاقَة (ṣadāqa).

def: n. Time immemorial. sadāmat se , adv. From time immemorial.
adv. = sadā, q.v.
more: adv. (dakh.) for سدا sadā, q. v.

def: s.m. A seat; dwelling, residence, house, palace, mansion; — perishing, decaying; exhaustion.
more: s. m. A place, house, mansion.
ex: سدن میں موجود ہر ایک شخص کو خوش آمدید
Welcome to every member presented in the chamber.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit सदन (sadana), ultimately from Proto-Indo-European *sed-.

def: s. (dakh.) Accomplishing, practising: see سادهنا sādhnā.
v. a. (dakh.) See سدهانا sadhānā.
adj. See s.v. sadā.
(dakh.) A flat ring, one inch and a half broad, worn round the waist by women ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
n. m. Principal Sadar amīn , now called a subordinate Judge.
adj. & adv. Having or paying respect, respectful; impassioned; — with respect, respectfully, considerately.

ety: for A. صدارة, v.n. fr. صدر; see ṣadr
def: s.f. The office of prime minister, or of chief justice; premiership; chief-justiceship.
more: (from صدر) s. f. The office of prime minister, or of chief justice, the chancellorship.
